mnemonics: HIDE AND SEEK: Alternative approaches are like a game of HIDE (Hardware-based, Information-centric, Distributed, Economic) and SEEK (Software-defined, End-to-end, Economic, Knowledge-centric) to find the best security solution.
keyPoints: Alternative approaches move beyond traditional perimeter-based security models.

They include paradigms like Software-Defined Networking (SDN) for centralized control, information-centric security focusing on data protection, and zero-trust architectures that assume no implicit trust.
These approaches often leverage hardware-based security (e.g., TPMs) and economic or behavioral models to improve security posture.
commonMistakes: Assuming alternative approaches completely replace traditional methods instead of complementing them.
Overlooking the complexity and initial setup cost of implementing a new paradigm like zero-trust.
Focusing solely on the technological shift and ignoring the necessary cultural and policy changes within an organization.
